claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a comprehensive harness boundary audit system that detects project file mutations using SHA256 snapshots and blocks PRD readiness on violations, with configurable agent tool profiles and cross-check evidence policy.

evidence.summary: Defines DEMAND_EVIDENCE_DISPATCH_SCHEMA_VERSION='1.0' and schema='yolo.demand.evidence_dispatch.v1'
evidence.why: Evidence dispatch uses versioned schema for compatibility checks

evidence.summary: Defines DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS with CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS including Read, Write, Edit, Bash, WebFetch, Agent, etc.
evidence.why: Boundary settings allow all major Claude tools during evidence collection, relying on post-execution audit rather than tool restrictions

evidence.summary: fileDigest function computes SHA256 hash of file contents
evidence.why: Boundary audit uses cryptographic digests to detect mutations, not just timestamps or size

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures SHA256 hashes of all project files excluding allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Pre-execution snapshot establishes baseline for boundary detection

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots and returns added/modified/deleted changes
evidence.why: Post-execution audit detects any project mutations by evidence agents

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation='forbidden', writes_business_code=false, and agent_instruction='boundary_controlled'
evidence.why: Explicit execution policy forbids mutations, but relies on boundary audit for enforcement

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes hard rules: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Return one JSON object only'
evidence.why: Agent prompts explicitly instruct evidence agents to avoid mutations and return structured JSON

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'research', 'full', or 'boundary' and configures Claude settings
evidence.why: Agent tool profiles control tool availability while boundary audit controls mutation enforcement

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshot before agent execution and diff after, blocking PRD readiness on mutations
evidence.why: Full boundary audit workflow from snapshot to blocker injection

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundaryBefore snapshot, runs agents, captures boundaryAfter, computes diff, and produces BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: Active boundary enforcement occurs during agent execution, not just in schema

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers array created with code='BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION' and message describing the violating file path and change type
evidence.why: Boundary violations become explicit blockers that prevent PRD readiness

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier with writes_code=false for all roles
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocols explicitly prohibit code writing by design

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns ['explorer','cross-checker','verifier'] for cross_check policy and ['explorer','verifier'] for single_agent
evidence.why: Cross-check evidence policy requires three-agent validation, not single explorer

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks that each required evidence agent role has at least one complete result and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues
evidence.why: PRD readiness validation enforces evidence completion before proceeding

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts between agents (block vs proceed or proceed vs clarify) and returns status='blocked' with conflicts array
evidence.why: Evidence disagreement becomes a blocker, preventing PRD readiness

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with tools, permissions, and settings
evidence.why: Provider adapter translates evidence dispatch configuration into actual provider CLI invocations

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt spawns provider CLI, pipes prompt to stdin, collects stdout/stderr, and returns structured result
evidence.why: Evidence agents are spawned as subprocesses with timeout and output capture

evidence.summary: Test verifies existing project field changes route to careful with cross_check evidence policy and needed_evidence_agents=['explorer','cross-checker','verifier']
evidence.why: Router tests confirm technical risk triggers cross-check evidence policy

evidence.summary: Test confirms cross-check PRD readiness requires all three evidence agent results before prd_ready=true
evidence.why: Test validates evidence protocol blocks PRD readiness on incomplete results

evidence.summary: Test verifies evidence protocols define writes_code=false, cross_checker required_when='cross_check', and conflicts block PRD readiness
evidence.why: Unit test confirms protocol contract and conflict detection

evidence.summary: Test verifies buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan returns status='ready', actions=['explorer','cross-checker','verifier'], and execution_policy with default_mode='dry_run'
evidence.why: Dispatch plan tests confirm schema structure and execution policy defaults

evidence.summary: Test executes explorer, cross-checker, and verifier agents then feeds results into readiness check
evidence.why: Integration test confirms full dispatch workflow from agent execution to readiness validation

evidence.summary: Test confirms claude dispatch keeps tools available (claude_allowed_tools includes WebFetch, Write, Bash) and boundary settings allow these tools
evidence.why: Test validates boundary control comes from snapshot audit, not tool restrictions

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, producing BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Critical boundary audit test confirms post-execution file diff blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: Package.json exports SDK entries for runtime/execution/provider-adapter and demand modules, with test script using node --import tsx --test
evidence.why: Project structure confirms evidence-dispatch and provider-adapter are exported SDK modules

risks: If boundary snapshot misses edge cases (symlinks, special files, race conditions during agent execution), mutations could go undetected
If provider adapter subprocess escapes or uses aliases not captured in snapshot, boundary audit might not detect real mutations
If full-tool agents modify files during evidence collection and then revert changes before snapshot, boundary audit would not detect the temporary mutation
missing: No evidence found for boundary audit behavior with concurrent file system operations outside agent control
No evidence found for boundary behavior when agents use external tools or subprocesses that might bypass direct file write detection
No evidence found for how boundary audit handles symbolic links, hard links, or special file types

result.notes: Primary claim verified by code and tests: boundary audit uses SHA256 snapshots to detect mutations and blocks PRD readiness on violations. Cross-check evidence policy requires three-agent validation (explorer, cross-checker, verifier). Agent tool profiles (boundary, research, full) control tool availability while boundary audit controls mutation enforcement. Test coverage confirms key behaviors including mutation detection, conflict blocking, and PRD readiness enforcement.
